records.question: Name schools in Riverside which the average of average math score for SAT is grater than 400, what is the funding type of these schools?
records.gold_sql: SELECT T1.sname, T2.`Charter Funding Type` FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.`District Name` LIKE 'Riverside%' GROUP BY T1.sname, T2.`Charter Funding Type` HAVING CAST(SUM(T1.AvgScrMath) AS REAL) / COUNT(T1.cds) > 400
records.baseline_pred: SELECT s.FundingType FROM schools AS s JOIN satscores AS t ON s.CDSCode = t.cds WHERE s.City = 'Riverside' AND t.AvgScrMath > 400 GROUP BY s.FundingType HAVING AVG(t.AvgScrMath) > 400
records.alt_pred: SELECT DISTINCT s.School AS school_name, s.FundingType AS funding_type FROM schools AS s INNER JOIN satscores AS ss ON s.CDSCode = ss.cds WHERE s.County = 'Riverside' AND ss.rtype = 'S' AND ss.AvgScrMath > 400

records.question: What is the complete address of the school with the lowest excellence rate? Indicate the Street, City, Zip and State.
records.gold_sql: SELECT T2.Street, T2.City, T2.State, T2.Zip FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode ORDER BY CAST(T1.NumGE1500 AS REAL) / T1.NumTstTakr ASC LIMIT 1
records.baseline_pred: SELECT Street, City, Zip, State FROM schools WHERE CDSCode = (SELECT cds FROM satscores WHERE NumTstTakr > 0 ORDER BY (NumGE1500 * 1.0 / NumTstTakr) ASC LIMIT 1)
records.alt_pred: SELECT schools."Street", schools."City", schools."Zip", schools."State" FROM schools INNER JOIN satscores ON schools."CDSCode" = satscores."cds" ORDER BY CAST(satscores."NumGE1500" AS REAL) / satscores."NumTstTakr" ASC LIMIT 1

records.question: For loans contracts which are still running where client are in debt, list the district of the and the state the percentage unemployment rate increment from year 1995 to 1996.
records.gold_sql: SELECT CAST((T3.A13 - T3.A12) AS REAL) * 100 / T3.A12 FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.district_id = T3.district_id WHERE T1.status = 'D'
records.baseline_pred: SELECT district.A2, CAST((district.A13 - district.A12) * 100.0 / district.A12 AS REAL) FROM loan JOIN account ON loan.account_id = account.account_id JOIN client ON account.district_id = client.district_id JOIN district ON client.district_id = district.district_id WHERE loan.status = 'D'
records.alt_pred: SELECT account_id FROM loan WHERE status = 'D') SELECT d.A2 AS district, ((d.A13 - d.A12) / d.A12) * 100 AS unemployment_rate_increment FROM debt_loans dl JOIN account a ON dl.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id

records.question: What elements are in a double type bond?
records.gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.element FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN bond AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id INNER JOIN connected AS T3 ON T1.atom_id = T3.atom_id WHERE T2.bond_type = '='
records.baseline_pred: SELECT DISTINCT a.element FROM atom a JOIN bond b ON a.molecule_id = b.molecule_id JOIN connected c ON b.bond_id = c.bond_id WHERE b.bond_type = '='
records.alt_pred: SELECT DISTINCT a.element FROM atom AS a INNER JOIN connected AS c ON a.atom_id = c.atom_id OR a.atom_id = c.atom_id2 INNER JOIN bond AS b ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id WHERE b.bond_type = '='

records.question: Name the card and artist with the most ruling information. Also state if the card is a promotional printing.
records.gold_sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.artist, T1.isPromo FROM cards AS T1 INNER JOIN rulings AS T2 ON T1.uuid = T2.uuid WHERE T1.isPromo = 1 AND T1.artist = (SELECT artist FROM cards WHERE isPromo = 1 GROUP BY artist HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT uuid) = (SELECT MAX(count_uuid) FROM ( SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT uuid) AS count_uuid FROM cards WHERE isPromo = 1 GROUP BY artist ))) LIMIT 1
records.baseline_pred: SELECT c.name, c.artist, c.isPromo FROM cards AS c JOIN rulings AS r ON c.uuid = r.uuid GROUP BY c.uuid ORDER BY COUNT(r.id) DESC LIMIT 1
records.alt_pred: SELECT uuid FROM rulings GROUP BY uuid ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT c.name, c.artist, c.isPromo FROM cards AS c INNER JOIN top_ruling_card AS t ON c.uuid = t.uuid

records.question: How many unknown power cards contain info about the triggered ability
records.gold_sql: SELECT Count(DISTINCT T1.id) FROM cards AS T1 INNER JOIN rulings AS T2 ON T1.uuid = T2.uuid WHERE (T1.power IS NULL OR T1.power = '*') AND T2.text LIKE '%triggered ability%'
records.baseline_pred: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cards WHERE (power IS NULL OR power = '*') AND text LIKE '%triggered ability%'
records.alt_pred: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cards WHERE (power IS NULL OR power = '*') AND text LIKE '%triggered ability%'

records.question: Please list the Italian names of the cards in the set Coldsnap with the highest converted mana cost.
records.gold_sql: SELECT T2.name FROM foreign_data AS T1 INNER JOIN cards AS T2 ON T2.uuid = T1.uuid INNER JOIN sets AS T3 ON T3.code = T2.setCode WHERE T3.name = 'Coldsnap' AND T1.language = 'Italian' ORDER BY T2.convertedManaCost DESC
records.baseline_pred: SELECT fd.name FROM cards AS c JOIN foreign_data AS fd ON c.uuid = fd.uuid JOIN sets AS s ON c.setCode = s.code WHERE s.name = 'Coldsnap' AND fd.language = 'Italian' ORDER BY c.convertedManaCost DESC LIMIT 1
records.alt_pred: SELECT DISTINCT fd.name FROM cards c INNER JOIN foreign_data fd ON fd.uuid = c.uuid WHERE c.setCode = 'CSP' AND fd.language = 'Italian' AND c.convertedManaCost = (SELECT MAX(c2.convertedManaCost) FROM cards c2 INNER JOIN foreign_data fd2 ON fd2.uuid = c2.uuid WHERE c2.setCode = 'CSP' AND fd2.language = 'Italian')

records.question: Write all the comments left by users who edited the post titled 'Why square the difference instead of taking the absolute value in standard deviation?'
records.gold_sql: SELECT T2.Comment FROM posts AS T1 INNER JOIN postHistory AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.PostId WHERE T1.Title = 'Why square the difference instead of taking the absolute value in standard deviation?'
records.baseline_pred: SELECT comments.Text FROM comments JOIN posts ON comments.PostId = posts.Id WHERE posts.Title = 'Why square the difference instead of taking the absolute value in standard deviation?'
records.alt_pred: SELECT DISTINCT comments.Text FROM comments INNER JOIN posts ON comments.PostId = posts.Id WHERE posts.Title = 'Why square the difference instead of taking the absolute value in standard deviation?'

records.question: Which user have only one post history per post and having at least 1000 views?
records.gold_sql: SELECT T2.UserId FROM users AS T1 INNER JOIN postHistory AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.UserId INNER JOIN posts AS T3 ON T2.PostId = T3.Id WHERE T3.ViewCount >= 1000 GROUP BY T2.UserId HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T2.PostHistoryTypeId) = 1
records.baseline_pred: SELECT DISTINCT ph.UserId FROM postHistory ph JOIN posts p ON ph.PostId = p.Id WHERE p.ViewCount >= 1000 AND ph.UserId IS NOT NULL GROUP BY ph.UserId, ph.PostId HAVING COUNT(*) = 1
records.alt_pred: SELECT DISTINCT ph.UserId FROM postHistory ph JOIN posts p ON ph.PostId = p.Id WHERE p.ViewCount >= 1000 GROUP BY ph.UserId, ph.PostId HAVING COUNT(*) = 1

records.question: Provide the text of the latest 10 comments to the post with the title 'Analysing wind data with R' and the display name of the user who left it.
records.gold_sql: SELECT T3.Text, T1.DisplayName FROM users AS T1 INNER JOIN posts AS T2 ON T1.Id = T2.OwnerUserId INNER JOIN comments AS T3 ON T2.Id = T3.PostId WHERE T2.Title = 'Analysing wind data with R' ORDER BY T1.CreationDate DESC LIMIT 10
records.baseline_pred: SELECT comments.Text, comments.UserDisplayName FROM comments JOIN posts ON comments.PostId = posts.Id WHERE posts.Title = 'Analysing wind data with R' ORDER BY comments.CreationDate DESC LIMIT 10
records.alt_pred: SELECT Id FROM posts WHERE Title = 'Analysing wind data with R' LIMIT 1), latest_comments AS (SELECT Text, UserId, CreationDate FROM comments WHERE PostId = (SELECT Id FROM target_post) ORDER BY CreationDate DESC LIMIT 10) SELECT latest_comments.Text, users.DisplayName FROM latest_comments LEFT JOIN users ON latest_comments.UserId = users.Id ORDER BY latest_comments.CreationDate DESC

records.question: What is the percentage of superheroes who act in their own self-interest or make decisions based on their own moral code? Indicate how many of the said superheroes were published by Marvel Comics.
records.gold_sql: SELECT (CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) * 100 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM superhero)), CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN publisher AS T2 ON T1.publisher_id = T2.id INNER JOIN alignment AS T3 ON T3.id = T1.alignment_id WHERE T3.alignment = 'Bad'
records.baseline_pred: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(s.id) AS percentage, SUM(CASE WHEN a.alignment = 'Bad' AND p.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM superhero s INNER JOIN alignment a ON s.alignment_id = a.id LEFT JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id
records.alt_pred: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(a.alignment = 'Bad',1,0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(s.id) AS percentage_bad, SUM(IIF(a.alignment = 'Bad' AND p.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics',1,0)) AS bad_published_by_marvel FROM superhero AS s LEFT JOIN alignment AS a ON s.alignment_id = a.id LEFT JOIN publisher AS p ON s.publisher_id = p.id

records.question: What is the best lap time recorded? List the driver and race with such recorded lap time.
records.gold_sql: SELECT T2.milliseconds, T1.forename, T1.surname, T3.name FROM drivers AS T1 INNER JOIN lapTimes AS T2 ON T1.driverId = T2.driverId INNER JOIN races AS T3 ON T2.raceId = T3.raceId ORDER BY T2.milliseconds ASC LIMIT 1
records.baseline_pred: SELECT drivers.forename, drivers.surname, races.name FROM lapTimes JOIN drivers ON lapTimes.driverId = drivers.driverId JOIN races ON lapTimes.raceId = races.raceId WHERE lapTimes.milliseconds = (SELECT MIN(milliseconds) FROM lapTimes) LIMIT 1
records.alt_pred:
SELECT d.forename, d.surname, r.name
FROM lapTimes lt
JOIN drivers d ON lt.driverId = d.driverId
JOIN races r ON lt.raceId = r.raceId
WHERE lt.milliseconds = (SELECT MIN(milliseconds) FROM lapTimes)

records.question: Calculate the percentage whereby Hamilton was not at the 1st track of the the f1 circuit since 2010.
records.gold_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.position <> 1 THEN T2.position END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T2.driverStandingsId) FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T3.surname = 'Hamilton' AND T1.year >= 2010
records.baseline_pred: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN r.positionOrder > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) FROM results r INNER JOIN drivers d ON r.driverId = d.driverId INNER JOIN races ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId WHERE d.surname = 'Hamilton' AND ra.year >= 2010
records.alt_pred: SELECT CAST((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results AS r INNER JOIN drivers AS d ON r.driverId = d.driverId INNER JOIN races AS ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId WHERE d.surname = 'Hamilton' AND ra.year >= 2010 AND r.position > 1 ) AS REAL) * 100 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results AS r INNER JOIN drivers AS d ON r.driverId = d.driverId INNER JOIN races AS ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId WHERE d.surname = 'Hamilton' AND ra.year >= 2010)

records.question: Which race was Alex Yoong in when he was in track number less than 20?
records.gold_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN driverStandings AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T3.forename = 'Alex' AND T3.surname = 'Yoong' AND T2.position < 20
records.baseline_pred: SELECT races.name FROM races JOIN results ON races.raceId = results.raceId JOIN drivers ON results.driverId = drivers.driverId WHERE drivers.forename = 'Alex' AND drivers.surname = 'Yoong' AND results.position < 20
records.alt_pred: SELECT DISTINCT r.name FROM races r INNER JOIN results res ON r.raceId = res.raceId INNER JOIN drivers d ON d.driverId = res.driverId WHERE d.forename = 'Alex' AND d.surname = 'Yoong' AND res.position < 20

records.question: In which Formula_1 race did Lewis Hamilton rank the highest?
records.gold_sql: SELECT name FROM races WHERE raceId IN ( SELECT raceId FROM results WHERE rank = 1 AND driverId = ( SELECT driverId FROM drivers WHERE forename = 'Lewis' AND surname = 'Hamilton' ) )
records.baseline_pred: SELECT races.name FROM races JOIN results ON races.raceId = results.raceId JOIN drivers ON results.driverId = drivers.driverId WHERE drivers.forename = 'Lewis' AND drivers.surname = 'Hamilton' ORDER BY results.rank ASC LIMIT 1
records.alt_pred: SELECT ra.name FROM results AS r INNER JOIN drivers AS d ON r.driverId = d.driverId INNER JOIN races AS ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId WHERE d.forename = 'Lewis' AND d.surname = 'Hamilton' ORDER BY r.rank ASC, ra.date ASC LIMIT 1

records.question: What is the fastest lap number of the champion in 2009?
records.gold_sql: SELECT T1.fastestLap FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 on T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T2.year = 2009 AND T1.time LIKE '_:%:__.___'
records.baseline_pred: SELECT lapTimes.lap FROM lapTimes JOIN races ON lapTimes.raceId = races.raceId JOIN results ON lapTimes.raceId = results.raceId AND lapTimes.driverId = results.driverId WHERE races.year = 2009 AND results.positionOrder = 1 AND lapTimes.time LIKE '%:%:%' ORDER BY lapTimes.milliseconds ASC LIMIT 1
records.alt_pred: SELECT res.fastestLap FROM results res JOIN races r ON res.raceId = r.raceId WHERE r.year = 2009 AND res.driverId = (SELECT ds.driverId FROM driverStandings ds JOIN races r2 ON ds.raceId = r2.raceId WHERE r2.year = 2009 AND ds.position = 1) AND res.fastestLap IS NOT NULL ORDER BY res.fastestLap ASC LIMIT 1

records.question: What are the speed in which attacks are put together of the top 4 teams with the highest build Up Play Speed?
records.gold_sql: SELECT t1.buildUpPlaySpeed FROM Team_Attributes AS t1 INNER JOIN Team AS t2 ON t1.team_api_id = t2.team_api_id ORDER BY t1.buildUpPlaySpeed ASC LIMIT 4
records.baseline_pred: SELECT buildUpPlaySpeed FROM Team_Attributes ORDER BY buildUpPlaySpeed DESC LIMIT 4
records.alt_pred: SELECT ta.buildUpPlaySpeed FROM Team_Attributes AS ta ORDER BY ta.buildUpPlaySpeed DESC LIMIT 4

records.question: How much higher in percentage is Ariel Borysiuk's overall rating than that of Paulin Puel?
records.gold_sql: SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Ariel Borysiuk' THEN t2.overall_rating ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 - SUM(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Paulin Puel' THEN t2.overall_rating ELSE 0 END)) * 100 / SUM(CASE WHEN t1.player_name = 'Paulin Puel' THEN t2.overall_rating ELSE 0 END) FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id
records.baseline_pred: SELECT CAST((MAX(CASE WHEN p.player_name = 'Ariel Borysiuk' THEN pa.overall_rating END) - MAX(CASE WHEN p.player_name = 'Paulin Puel' THEN pa.overall_rating END)) * 100.0 / MAX(CASE WHEN p.player_name = 'Paulin Puel' THEN pa.overall_rating END) AS REAL) AS percentage_higher FROM Player p INNER JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id WHERE p.player_name IN ('Ariel Borysiuk', 'Paulin Puel') AND pa.overall_rating IS NOT NULL
records.alt_pred: WITH A AS (SELECT PA.overall_rating AS a_rating FROM Player AS P JOIN Player_Attributes AS PA ON P.player_api_id = PA.player_api_id WHERE P.player_name = 'Ariel Borysiuk' ORDER BY PA.date DESC LIMIT 1), B AS (SELECT PA.overall_rating AS b_rating FROM Player AS P JOIN Player_Attributes AS PA ON P.player_api_id = PA.player_api_id WHERE P.player_name = 'Paulin Puel' ORDER BY PA.date DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT CASE WHEN B.b_rating IS NULL OR B.b_rating = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (CAST(A.a_rating - B.b_rating AS REAL) / B.b_rating) * 100 END AS percent_higher FROM A CROSS JOIN B

records.question: Please state the finishing rate and curve score of the player who has the heaviest weight.
records.gold_sql: SELECT id, finishing, curve FROM Player_Attributes WHERE player_api_id = ( SELECT player_api_id FROM Player ORDER BY weight DESC LIMIT 1 ) LIMIT 1
records.baseline_pred: SELECT finishing, curve FROM Player_Attributes JOIN Player ON Player_Attributes.player_api_id = Player.player_api_id WHERE Player.weight = (SELECT MAX(weight) FROM Player)
records.alt_pred: SELECT pa.finishing, pa.curve FROM Player_Attributes pa INNER JOIN Player p ON pa.player_api_id = p.player_api_id WHERE p.weight = (SELECT MAX(weight) FROM Player)

records.question: The oldest SJS patient's medical laboratory work was completed on what date, and what age was the patient when they initially arrived at the hospital?
records.gold_sql: SELECT T1.Date, STRFTIME('%Y', T2.`First Date`) - STRFTIME('%Y', T2.Birthday),T2.Birthday FROM Laboratory AS T1 INNER JOIN Patient AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.Diagnosis = 'SJS' AND T2.Birthday IS NOT NULL ORDER BY T2.Birthday ASC LIMIT 1
records.baseline_pred: SELECT L.Date, (strftime('%Y', P."First Date") - strftime('%Y', P.Birthday)) FROM Patient P INNER JOIN Laboratory L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE P.Diagnosis = 'SJS' AND P.Birthday = (SELECT MIN(Birthday) FROM Patient WHERE Diagnosis = 'SJS') ORDER BY L.Date ASC LIMIT 1
records.alt_pred: SELECT l.Date, CAST(strftime('%Y', p."First Date") AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', p.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age_when_arrived FROM Patient p JOIN Examination e ON e.ID = p.ID JOIN Laboratory l ON l.ID = p.ID WHERE p.Diagnosis = 'SJS' ORDER BY p.Birthday ASC, l.Date ASC LIMIT 1

records.question: Was the patient with the number 57266's uric acid within a normal range?
records.gold_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (T1.SEX = 'F' AND T2.UA > 6.5) OR (T1.SEX = 'M' AND T2.UA > 8.0) THEN true ELSE false END FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.ID = 57266
records.baseline_pred: SELECT CASE WHEN (T1.UA > 8.0 AND T2.SEX = 'M') OR (T1.UA > 6.5 AND T2.SEX = 'F') THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS is_normal FROM Laboratory AS T1 INNER JOIN Patient AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.ID = 57266 LIMIT 1
records.alt_pred: SELECT P.SEX, L.UA FROM Laboratory AS L JOIN Patient AS P ON L.ID = P.ID WHERE P.ID = 57266 ORDER BY L.Date DESC LIMIT 1) SELECT CASE WHEN SEX = 'M' AND UA > 8.0 THEN 1 WHEN SEX = 'F' AND UA > 6.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS within_normal_range FROM latest

records.question: Among the male patients who have a normal level of white blood cells, how many of them have an abnormal fibrinogen level?
records.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.FG <= 150 OR T2.FG >= 450 AND T2.WBC > 3.5 AND T2.WBC < 9.0 AND T1.SEX = 'M'
records.baseline_pred: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT L.ID) FROM Patient P INNER JOIN Laboratory L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE P.SEX = 'M' AND L.WBC > 3.5 AND L.WBC < 9.0 AND (L.FG <= 150 OR L.FG >= 450)
records.alt_pred: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT "Patient"."ID" FROM "Patient" INNER JOIN "Laboratory" ON "Patient"."ID" = "Laboratory"."ID" WHERE "Patient"."SEX" = 'M' AND "Laboratory"."WBC" > 3.5 AND "Laboratory"."WBC" < 9.0 AND ("Laboratory"."FG" <= 150 OR "Laboratory"."FG" >= 450)) AS T

records.question: How many patients with an Ig G higher than normal?
records.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID INNER JOIN Examination AS T3 ON T3.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.IGG >= 2000
records.baseline_pred: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT ID) FROM Laboratory WHERE IGG >= 2000
records.alt_pred: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT T1.ID FROM Laboratory AS T1 WHERE T1.IGG >= 2000)

records.question: How many patients with a normal Ig A level came to the hospital after 1990/1/1?
records.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.IGA BETWEEN 80 AND 500 AND  strftime('%Y',  T1.`First Date`) > '1990'
records.baseline_pred: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT P.ID) FROM Patient P JOIN Laboratory L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE L.IGA > 80 AND L.IGA < 500 AND P.`First Date` >= '1990-01-01'
records.alt_pred: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Patient WHERE ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT T1.ID FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.IGA > 80 AND T2.IGA < 500) AND `First Date` >= '1990-01-01'

records.question: Among the patients who has a normal level of anti-centromere and a normal level of anti-SSB, how many of them are male?
records.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.CENTROMEA IN ('negative', '0') AND T2.SSB IN ('negative', '0') AND T1.SEX = 'M'
records.baseline_pred: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT P.ID) FROM Patient P JOIN Examination E ON P.ID = E.ID WHERE E.CENTROMEA IN ('-', '+-') AND E.SSB IN ('-', '+-') AND P.SEX = 'M'
records.alt_pred: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT L.ID FROM Laboratory AS L INNER JOIN Patient AS P ON L.ID = P.ID WHERE L.CENTROMEA IN ('-','+-') AND L.SSB IN ('-','+-') AND P.SEX = 'M')

records.question: Who is the top spending customer and how much is the average price per single item purchased by this customer? What currency was being used?
records.gold_sql: SELECT T2.CustomerID, SUM(T2.Price / T2.Amount), T1.Currency FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN transactions_1k AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T2.CustomerID = ( SELECT CustomerID FROM yearmonth ORDER BY Consumption DESC LIMIT 1 ) GROUP BY T2.CustomerID, T1.Currency
records.baseline_pred: SELECT customers.CustomerID, SUM(transactions_1k.Price) / SUM(transactions_1k.Amount) AS avg_price_per_item, customers.Currency FROM transactions_1k JOIN customers ON transactions_1k.CustomerID = customers.CustomerID GROUP BY transactions_1k.CustomerID, customers.Currency ORDER BY SUM(transactions_1k.Price) DESC LIMIT 1
records.alt_pred:
SELECT t.CustomerID, SUM(t.Amount * t.Price) AS total_spent
  FROM transactions_1k t
  GROUP BY t.CustomerID
), top_customer AS (
  SELECT CustomerID
  FROM customer_spending
  ORDER BY total_spent DESC
  LIMIT 1
), item_avg AS (
  SELECT t.CustomerID, SUM(t.Price) * 1.0 / SUM(t.Amount) AS avg_price_per_item
  FROM transactions_1k t
  JOIN top_customer tc ON t.CustomerID = tc.CustomerID
  GROUP BY t.CustomerID
)
SELECT c.CustomerID, ia.avg_price_per_item, c.Currency
FROM customers c
JOIN top_customer tc ON c.CustomerID = tc.CustomerID
JOIN item_avg ia ON c.CustomerID = ia.CustomerID
